relations: Odor molecules bind to olfactory receptors to initiate the sense of smell
Odor molecules indirectly cause the brain to recognize smells through the process involving olfactory receptors
Odor molecules enter the nasal cavity through the nostrils to begin the process of smell
Olfactory receptors are located within the olfactory epithelium
Olfactory receptors send signals to the brain to be interpreted as smells
The nose, through the nasal cavity, contains olfactory receptors for detecting smells
The nasal cavity houses the olfactory epithelium where olfactory receptors are located
The human body's brain processes and interprets signals from the olfactory system as specific smells
The nasal cavity is a part of the nose which plays a crucial role in the sense of smell
The nose is an organ of the human body specifically adapted for the sense of smell
The Proustian effect is named after Marcel Proust, who wrote about the phenomenon of smell triggering memories
Aromatherapy utilizes the Proustian effect by using scents to evoke positive emotions and memories for therapeutic purposes
Lavender is used in aromatherapy to aid in relaxation, showcasing the application of scent for psychological well-being
Peppermint is used in aromatherapy to enhance focus, demonstrating the use of scent for improving psychological and physical states
The fragrance industry tailors scents to align with cultural preferences and enhance social interactions
